heading: Advisory Council on Unemployment Compensation
label: 1108 Advisory Council on Unemployment Compensation

Termination of Advisory CouncilsAdvisory councils established after , to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period beginning on the date of their establishment, unless, in the case of a council established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such council is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a council established by the Congress, its duration is otherwise provided by law. See sections 1001(2) and 1013 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 5, 1973

Report on Agricultural Labor Performed by Aliens, , , directed Advisory Council on Unemployment Compensation to submit a report to Congress, not later than , on its recommendations with respect to the treatment of agricultural labor performed by aliens.Pub. children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: Not later than , and every 4th year thereafter, the Secretary of Labor shall establish an advisory council to be known as the Advisory Council on Unemployment Compensation (referred to in this section as the “Council”).February 1, 1992

children.heading: Function
children.label: b Function
children.text: It shall be the function of each Council to evaluate the unemployment compensation program, including the purpose, goals, countercyclical effectiveness, coverage, benefit adequacy, trust fund solvency, funding of State administrative costs, administrative efficiency, and any other aspects of the program and to make recommendations for improvement.

children.label: A
children.text: 5 members appointed by the President, to include representatives of business, labor, State government, and the public.

children.label: B
children.text: 3 members appointed by the President pro tempore of the Senate, in consultation with the Chairman and ranking member of the Committee on Finance of the Senate.

children.label: C
children.text: 3 members appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives, in consultation with the Chairman and ranking member of the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 representative of the interests of business,

children.label: B
children.text: 1 representative of the interests of labor, and

children.label: C
children.text: 1 representative of the interests of State governments.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 3 Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy in any Council shall be filled in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Chairman
children.label: 4 Chairman
children.text: The President shall appoint the Chairman of the Council from among its members.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each Council may engage any technical assistance (including actuarial services) required by the Council to carry out its functions under this section.

children.heading: Assistance from Secretary of Labor
children.label: 2 Assistance from Secretary of Labor
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall provide each Council with any staff, office facilities, and other assistance, and any data prepared by the Department of Labor, required by the Council to carry out its functions under this section.

shall be entitled to receive compensation at the rate of pay for level V of the Executive Schedule under for each day (including travel time) during which such member is engaged in the actual performance of duties vested in the Council, andsection 5316 of title 5

while engaged in the performance of such duties away from such member’s home or regular place of business, shall be allowed travel expenses (including per diem in lieu of subsistence) as authorized by for persons in the Government employed intermittently.section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than February 1 of the third year following the year in which any Council is required to be established under subsection (a), the Council shall submit to the President and the Congress a report setting forth the findings and recommendations of the Council as a result of its evaluation of the unemployment compensation program under this section.

children.heading: Report of first Council
children.label: 2 Report of first Council
children.text: The Council shall include in its report required to be submitted by , the Council’s findings and recommendations with respect to determining eligibility for extended unemployment benefits on the basis of unemployment statistics for regions, States, or subdivisions of States.February 1, 1995
